touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Jack O'Connor at CloudPrism. No answer, left voicemail introducing our conversation intelligence platform and mentioning we help eCommerce companies improve rep productivity.
Second cold call attempt to Jack O'Connor. No answer, no voicemail left this time.

Cold email sent to Jack O'Connor highlighting how similar eCommerce companies have improved forecast accuracy and rep ramp time. Mentioned case study with 40% reduction in onboarding time. No response received.

Brief connection call with Jack O'Connor. He expressed interest in exploring alternatives to their current processes. Confirmed he oversees RevOps and is looking at tools to help their growing sales team. Scheduled qualification call for next week.

questions_asked.question: What's driving you to look at conversation intelligence tools right now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling the sales team and need better visibility into what's working. Currently pretty manual.

next_steps: Send calendar invite for qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Jack O'Connor. Discussed current challenges with sales process visibility and rep coaching. Jack mentioned they're evaluating a couple of options including Gong. CloudPrism has 9 sales reps and wants to improve deal execution and reduce ramp time. Budget around $6-7K annually.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for coaching reps and reviewing calls?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly ad-hoc. Managers listen to a few calls when they have time, but there's no systematic approach.

questions_asked.question: What other solutions are you looking at?
questions_asked.answer: We've had a demo with Gong. They're the obvious choice but wondering if there are better fits for our size.

questions_asked.question: How does your automation tier compare to Gong's workflows?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited automation includes custom triggers, deal alerts, and automatic follow-up suggestions without per-user limits.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call with Elena Huang from his team to dive into technical requirements
Send ROI calculator tailored to eCommerce

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up discovery call with Jack O'Connor. Went deeper on use cases - primarily rep productivity, coaching consistency, and identifying at-risk deals earlier. Jack shared that Elena Huang will be evaluating the technical side. Discussed integration with their Salesforce instance and Slack workflows.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform track email conversations or just calls?
questions_asked.answer: Both - we process calls, emails, and can integrate with your calendar to provide complete account context.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this besides you and Elena?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, it's mostly me and Elena driving this. Our VP Sales trusts us to make the call on tools like this.

next_steps: Set up technical demo for Elena

touches.ai_summary: Proposal call with Jack O'Connor to present pricing and implementation plan. Walked through 9-seat quote at $9,200 ACV with unlimited automation tier. Jack expressed sticker shock and mentioned Gong came in lower. Discussed feature differences and value of automation capabilities for their use case.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down what we're paying for in the unlimited automation versus basic?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited tier includes custom workflows, real-time deal scoring, automatic coaching recommendations, and priority support - basic is capped at 5 workflows per user.

questions_asked.question: What did Gong's pricing look like for comparison?
questions_asked.answer: I can't discuss competitor specifics, but we're seeing customers choose us for the automation depth at SMB scale.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on price if we commit annually?
questions_asked.answer: The quote is already annual - let me talk to my manager about what we can do given you're comparing options.

objections: Price higher than expected compared to Gong's proposal
next_steps: Discuss discount options with sales leadership
Schedule follow-up to present revised pricing

touches.ai_summary: Extended negotiation call with Jack O'Connor. Presented revised proposal at $5,900 ACV (36% discount) to match budget constraints and stay competitive with Gong. Jack appreciated the flexibility but wants to review internally with Elena before committing. Discussed implementation timeline and onboarding support. Concerns about lack of exec involvement, but Jack assured he has authority to approve.

questions_asked.question: If we sign by end of month, can you include an extra training session for the team?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can add one additional onboarding workshop if we close this month.

questions_asked.question: Should we get your VP Sales on a call to align on success metrics?
questions_asked.answer: Not necessary - she's swamped with quota planning. I'll brief her, but this is my domain.

questions_asked.question: What's the cancellation policy if this doesn't work out after 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Annual contract, but we can discuss early termination clauses if performance benchmarks aren't met.

questions_asked.question: How is Elena feeling about the technical fit after her evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: She's comfortable with the integrations. Main thing is just getting the pricing right.

objections: Needs to stay within approved budget range
next_steps: Jack to review with Elena and finance by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Pricing finalization call with Jack O'Connor. Confirmed $5,900 ACV for 9 seats with unlimited automation. Walked through contract terms, payment schedule (annual upfront), and implementation kickoff timeline. Jack committed to getting contract signed within 5 business days pending final finance review.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Given the discount level, we need annual payment to make this work financially.

questions_asked.question: What's the internal approval process look like from here?
questions_asked.answer: Finance sign-off, which should be quick since it's under my approval threshold. Then we're good to go.

next_steps: Jack to route through finance for payment approval
Tentative kickoff call scheduled for following week
